notes: Ran it using Proton 4.2-3 in Lutris. You have to use the Uplay installer script providede by Lutris, and then install + run games from Uplay. Disabling Esync removed all sluggishness from the Uplay UI, and AC2 gameplay.
title: Assassin's Creed 2 Deluxe Edition

notes:
As someone wrote below- it works with Lutris. But it does not actually use Proton. It runs Steam in Wine environment where you download AC2 through uplay which also runs on wine.
Or I did it wrong ;).
Anyhow, there is a lot of tampering to be done before you get this running. For me it sometimes displays black screen in the game menu. I found that moving the window between displays helps in most of the cases. Sometimes you need to kill all wine processes in Lutris before it finally works.
Otherwise ok, doesn't crash.

notes: I bought the game on Steam, but I only get the message when starting the game "Failed to request product key from Steam, please try again later". So days later, still no product key, had to request a refund.
title: Assassin's Creed 2 Deluxe Edition

notes:
Before starting steam run: 
"sed -i 's/legacykeyregistrylocation/legacykeyregistrylocation2/g' ~/.steam/steam/appcache/appinfo.vdf" 
Then the game will start without giving the not loading key error.
title: Assassin's Creed 2 Deluxe Edition

notes:
you need to may this each time that begin steam and working good, something issues with fps but runs good

https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/190#issuecomment-513589006
title: Assassin's Creed 2 Deluxe Edition

notes: Ok, here is what you must do: 1. Create a file with no extension with any name with this script: "sed -i 's/legacykeyregistrylocation/legacykeyregistrylocation2/g' ~/.steam/steam/appcache/appinfo.vdf" without quotes. 2. Make it launch at startup of your system with startup manager. WARNING: DO NOT use it in /etc/init.d because steam launches as an user program, not as a root. 3. Right click your file => Properties and then "make this file executable". 4. Set the game launch options with: "PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%" without quotes. This will increase the performance drastically. Overall the gameplay has no issues, it runs great. The only issue is if you want to activate MSAA it must be in the main menu. If you do that during a game session the game will crash. 10/10, thanks github/valve
title: Assassin's Creed 2 Deluxe Edition

notes: As others have stated, using "sed -i 's/legacykeyregistrylocation/legacykeyregistrylocation2/g' ~/.steam/steam/appcache/appinfo.vdf" before starting Steam is necessary to bypass the key issue. Also Wine D9vk and disabling Esync are essential to having a good gameplay experience in my tests, but your mileage may very. Once all the tweaks are applied, the game runs no worse than on Windows. 60FPS with only minor stuttering when loading in some assets are expected.
title: Assassin's Creed 2 Deluxe Edition

notes:
Using the aforementioned appinfo.vdf adjustment gets the game running. However, performance is abysmal to the point of unplayable.

My card does not support D9VK. That would probably help, but tough luck.
title: Assassin's Creed 2 Deluxe Edition
